description: Run a proxy mainline TopK anchor at K=128 using Adam and a reduced LR, keeping architecture and sparsity fixed so the round cleanly tests whether optimizer retuning materially lowers FVU.
self_review: This is still a local optimization pass, so the gain may be limited if the quality ceiling is architectural rather than optimizer-driven. I am assuming the default 8e-4 is better suited to signum than Adam.

description: Retry the same K=128 TopK Adam anchor after a narrow startup fix in `sparsify/__main__.py` that skips unnecessary rank-by-rank artifact loading when the model and dataset paths are local.
self_review: This targets startup latency only, so it may not help if the watchdog is being tripped by a different pre-step bottleneck such as dataset shuffling or hook initialization. The quality hypothesis itself remains untested until the run reaches metrics.

description: Run a proxy `factorized_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether the added hidden mixing stage can beat the 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory on quality.
self_review: This introduces a new architecture family, so the first proxy result may underperform or show extra compute cost. If it is only marginally better than TopK, it still may not justify promotion given the aggressive goal of roughly halving the current baseline FVU.
